name: CAROLI DISEASE
definition: COMMUNICATING CAVERNOUS ECTASIA OF INTRAHEPATIC BILE DUCTS rare congenital autosomal recessive disorder characterized by multifocal segmental saccular both features of Caroli disease and congenital hepatic fibrosis arrest of bile duct remodeling during embryogenesis + during later development of more

demographics.Age: childhood + 2nd-3rd decade, occasionally in infancy; M÷F = 1÷1
demographics.Associated with: benign renal tubular ectasia, medullary sponge kidney (in 80%), infantile

dominant_finding: beaded ducts with alternating dilated and strictured segments
discriminating_features: multiple small localized / diffusely scattered cysts converging toward porta hepatis
